attributes.description: Alerts when pods are stuck in Pending phase (phase == 1). Pending pods cannot be scheduled — typically due to insufficient node resources, node affinity/taint mismatches, or missing PVCs. Sustained Pending pods are a proxy for scheduling latency.
attributes.name: [K8s OTel] Pods stuck in Pending phase

investigation_guide.blob:
## Pods Stuck in Pending Phase

### Triage Steps
1. Identify the affected pod(s) from the alert results.
2. Run `kubectl describe pod <pod_name> -n <namespace>` — the Events section shows why scheduling failed.
3. Check the Cluster Overview dashboard for node resource availability.
4. Verify node resources: `kubectl describe nodes | grep -A 5 'Allocated resources'`.
5. Check for PVC binding issues: `kubectl get pvc -n <namespace>`.

### Common Causes
- Insufficient CPU or memory on available nodes
- Node affinity, anti-affinity, or taint/toleration mismatches
- PersistentVolumeClaim not bound (no available PV)
- Pod topology spread constraints cannot be satisfied
- Resource quotas exceeded in the namespace

### Tuning
- The `alertDelay` of 5 runs (5 minutes at 1m interval) allows for normal scheduling delays.
- Increase `alertDelay` for clusters with slow autoscaling (e.g. cloud node provisioning).
- Add namespace filters to exclude namespaces where Pending pods are expected (e.g. batch workloads waiting for resources).

### Escalation
Sustained Pending pods indicate the cluster cannot meet workload demand. Consider scaling up the node pool or reviewing resource requests.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-k8sclusterreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE k8s.pod.phase IS NOT NULL
| STATS max_phase = MAX(k8s.pod.phase)
    BY k8s.pod.name, k8s.namespace.name, k8s.node.name
// Phase 1 = Pending — alert only if the pod never progressed beyond Pending
// in the window. If it reached Running (2) or beyond, MAX > 1 and it drops out.
| WHERE max_phase == 1
| EVAL status = "Pending"
| KEEP k8s.namespace.name, k8s.pod.name, k8s.node.name, status
| SORT k8s.namespace.name ASC, k8s.pod.name ASC
| LIMIT 100
